summary: Build and govern a 50-200 example domain-specific held-out benchmark. Distinct from public benchmarks (MMLU/HumanEval/GSM8K via lm-evaluation-harness) which measure general capability, not YOUR task. Only a held-out domain set predicts production behavior.

operations.name: SourceFromRealTraffic
operations.trigger: Need to know whether a system works on the actual domain; no curated eval set exists yet; production or pilot traffic is available.
operations.action: Sample real domain inputs (logs, tickets, queries, transactions) stratified by query type / segment / difficulty. Pull edge cases and failure reports deliberately, not just the head of the distribution.
operations.output: Raw candidate example pool (>= 2x target size) with provenance and timestamp per example.
operations.evidence: DSPy build-a-dev-set discipline [dspy.ai/learn/optimization/overview/]; LlamaIndex eval is built from the corpus, not synthetic in isolation (OP-10 EvalLoop).

operations.name: LabelAndCurate
operations.trigger: Raw candidate pool collected; need ground-truth or reference outputs.
operations.action: Attach gold answer / acceptable-reference / pass-fail label per example. Two annotators on a subset; resolve disagreement; drop ambiguous items. Record the rubric used. For RAG, label the gold passage too (for MRR/hit-rate).
operations.output: Curated labeled set with inter-annotator agreement noted; rejected/ambiguous items logged.
operations.evidence: DSPy Case C: spot-check metric vs human on >=20 cases before trusting it; LlamaIndex RetrieverEvaluator needs labeled gold passages [developers.llamaindex.ai/python/framework-api-reference/evaluation/].

operations.name: HoldOutDiscipline
operations.trigger: Labeled set exists and is about to be used.
operations.action: Split into train / dev / test (held-out). The held-out test set is NEVER shown to the optimizer, NEVER pasted into a prompt as a few-shot demo, NEVER used to pick chunk size / reranker / model. Store it separately; gate access.
operations.output: Sealed held-out test split + train/dev splits; access policy documented.
operations.evidence: DSPy: 'compiled program beats baseline on a held-out test set (not the val set used in optimization)' [dspy.ai/learn/optimization/overview/]; DSPy Case A step 4 requires held-out distinct from val.

operations.name: SizeFor50to200
operations.trigger: Deciding how many examples the held-out set needs.
operations.action: Target 50-200 domain examples. 50 = minimum for a coarse production signal; 100-200 = stable enough to detect ~5-10pp regressions and to stratify by segment. Below ~30, differences are noise. Size up if you need per-segment slices.
operations.output: Sized held-out set with per-segment counts.
operations.evidence: DSPy sweet spot: 30 minimum useful, 200+ required for MIPROv2 to avoid overfit [dspy.ai/learn/optimization/overview/]; LlamaIndex DatasetGenerator default num=50 [llamaindex SKILL OP-10].

operations.name: VersionTheSet
operations.trigger: Held-out set is finalized for a release cycle.
operations.action: Pin the set as a versioned artifact (eval_v1.jsonl) with a hash, a creation date, and the labeling rubric. Score every model/prompt/retriever change against the SAME version. Bump version only on a deliberate refresh.
operations.output: Versioned, hashed eval artifact checked into the repo; results table keyed by (artifact_version, system_version).
operations.evidence: DSPy ships program.json as a versioned artifact [dspy.ai/tutorials/saving/]; LlamaIndex versions indices as deployment artifacts (Stage 5).

operations.name: RefreshOnDomainShift
operations.trigger: Domain distribution drifts (new product, new user segment, seasonal change); held-out scores stop tracking production complaints.
operations.action: Add fresh real examples from recent traffic; retire stale ones; re-label edge cases that production surfaced. Bump the version. Keep the old version for back-comparison. Refresh cadence: quarterly or on any major domain change, whichever first.
operations.output: New eval version; drift log noting what changed and why.
operations.evidence: LlamaIndex live-corpus reconciliation (IngestionPipeline UPSERTS_AND_DELETE, A10); production post-mortems trace failures to stale eval not matching live distribution.

operations.name: LeakAudit
operations.trigger: Before any release; whenever few-shot demos or fine-tune data are assembled.
operations.action: Diff the held-out set against (a) few-shot prompt demos, (b) fine-tune / training data, (c) the DSPy trainset. Any overlap = contamination -> the held-out number is inflated and meaningless. Remove the overlap or rebuild the split.
operations.output: Leak-audit report: 0 overlap confirmed, or list of leaked items removed.
operations.evidence: DSPy held-out-distinct-from-val rule [dspy.ai/learn/optimization/overview/]; ML contamination literature; metric-design calibration-receipt provenance discipline (OP-M10).

operations.name: PairWithPublicBench
operations.trigger: A public-benchmark number (MMLU/HumanEval/GSM8K) is being used to justify a deployment decision.
operations.action: Treat the public bench as a capability floor / sanity check only. Require the domain held-out set as the decision gate. Report both side by side; if they disagree, the domain set wins for the go/no-go.
operations.output: Two-axis report: {public capability score, domain held-out score}; deployment gated on the latter.
operations.evidence: lm-evaluation-harness covers public academic benchmarks, not your domain [~/.claude/skills/lm-evaluation-harness]; public benches measure general capability not task fit.
